The minutes have been verified. The verification has been announced on the Alingsås Municipality's digital notice board.

Municipal Council Minutes

2026-06-15

Extract Confirmation

§ 112 2026.272 KS Budget 2027 and Frameworks 2028-2029 for the Alingsås and Vårgårda Fire and Rescue Service Association (hereinafter referred to as the "Association").

Case Description: The Board of Directors of the Alingsås and Vårgårda Fire and Rescue Service Association decided on April 17, 2026, § 10, to approve the 2027 budget, the framework budgets for 2028–2029, the investment budget for 2027–2031, and the proposed amendments.

Alingsås municipality's contribution is estimated at 69,994,000 SEK in 2027; 72,906,000 SEK in 2028; and 75,706,000 SEK in 2029.

Preparation: In a letter dated May 26, 2026, the Municipal Council provided the following statement:

When preparing the budget and frameworks, the Association based its calculations on 100 percent of the municipal activity price index (PKV as of February). This corresponds to an increase of 3.0 percent in 2027; 3.7 percent in 2028; and 3.4 percent in 2029.

In addition to the PKV adjustment, the frameworks have been adjusted for:

  • 2027: In accordance with the strategic decision of 2026-02-20, the Association will continue working on a joint management system, including the current Älvsborg, Södra Halland, and Fyrbodal regions; 1,000,000 SEK.
  • 2028 and 2029: Increased costs for SWEN (Swedish Emergency Network), which replaces the current RAKEL (Radio Communication for Efficient Management) system; 830,000 SEK (415,000 SEK per year).

The statutory requirement for good financial management applies to both operations and finances. The financial target for good financial management in 2027 is a positive result of 1 percent of the turnover (revenues + membership contributions).

The total operating costs are estimated at 90,362,000 SEK in 2027; 94,120,000 SEK in 2028; and 97,735,000 SEK in 2029, and are distributed among the member municipalities based on population size. Alingsås municipality's share/membership contribution is estimated at 77.46 percent.

The investment budget for the years 2027–2031 is detailed in the supporting documents for the case. During this period, investments totaling 38,211,000 SEK are planned, with the largest investments being a high-reach vehicle in 2027 (10,000,000 SEK) and a fire engine in 2028 (7,500,000 SEK). In addition, investments are planned for a transport vehicle, towing vehicle, water pumps, a boat, and investments in technical equipment related to the SWEN communication system.

The Municipal Council's working committee addressed the case on June 3, 2026, § 84.

Municipal Board Minutes

2026-06-15

Confirmation of Extract

Decision Proposed Resolution for the Municipal Council The budget for 2027 and the framework for 2028-2029, as well as the investment budget for 2027-2031, for the Alingsås and Vårgårda Fire and Rescue Service Association (AVRF) are approved.

Supporting Documents

  • §84 KSAU: Budget 2027 and framework 2028-2029 for AVRF
  • Service Report: Budget 2027 and framework 2028-2029 for Alingsås and Vårgårda Fire and Rescue Service Association
  • §10 AVRF: Budget 2027 and framework 2028-2029
  • Budget 2027, framework 2028-2029, and investment budget 2027-2031, adopted version

Municipal Board Minutes

2026-06-15

Confirmation of Extract

§ 113 2026.266 KS Review of Parking Fines

Description of the Issue The document "Parking Fines in the Municipality of Alingsås" lists types of violations governed by the Swedish Transport Agency's regulations regarding parking notices and parking fines (TSFS 2024:57), which were adopted on August 28, 2024.

The parking fines in the Municipality of Alingsås were last decided by the Municipal Council on September 27, 2023, § 165, and need to be reviewed. This current review is due to changes introduced by TSFS 2024:57. The review does not involve any new decisions regarding the amounts of the fines.

The Technical Committee addressed this issue on May 18, 2026, § 28.

Preparation The Municipal Planning Department provided the following statement in a letter dated April 28, 2026:

Sections of the document that have been revised based on TSFS 2024:57:

  • Heading "Parking Violations," bullet list, addition to the fee of 600 SEK: "or if other conditions are not met." The text has been updated to facilitate the assessment of different types of violations.
  • Heading "Parking Violations," bullet list, addition to the fee of 400 SEK: "or if the conditions for the fee are not met." The text has been updated to facilitate the assessment of different types of violations.

Types of Violations

  • 07: A portion of the text that was repeated in the next line (08) has been removed.
  • 09: An addition referencing the Traffic Code has been made, and a fee of 1000 SEK has been removed.
  • 21: A portion of the text has been removed and moved to a new line (22).
  • 27: The text has been changed to "on a bicycle street (8 kap. 1 a §)."
  • 28: A new line has been added for the type of violation. The Committee on Working Matters has addressed this issue during its meeting on May 11, § 19.

The Municipal Board's Committee on Working Matters addressed this issue on June 3, 2026, § 86.

Decision Proposed Decision for the Municipal Council The parking fines in the Municipality of Alingsås are approved.

Supporting Documents

  • §86 KSAU: Review of parking fines
  • § 28 TEN: Review of parking fines
  • Review of parking fines

Municipal Board Minutes

2026-06-15

Confirmation of Extract

§ 114 2026.288 KS Proposal to the Municipal Board regarding an investigation into expanded parking capacity at Nolhaga - Jan-Erik Wallin (M) and others.

Description of the matter: According to the Municipal Act, Chapter 4, Section 20, each member of a committee has the right to propose a matter.

On June 1, 2026, Jan-Erik Wallin (M), Lady France Mulumba (KD), and Jens Christian Berlin (L) submitted a proposal to the Municipal Board, § 110, regarding an investigation into expanded parking capacity at Nolhaga.

The proposal suggests that the Municipal Board task the municipal planning department with promptly investigating the possibility of expanding parking capacity at Nolhaga by constructing a multi-story parking garage on the existing parking area. The investigation should present possible solutions, estimated capacity increase, investment costs, and feasibility.

Proposal for a decision at the meeting: Jan-Erik Wallin (M) and Lady France Mulumba (KD) propose that the proposal be referred to the municipal management office for preparation.

Anton César (S), Anita Hedén Unosson (S), Peter Norlander (S), Pamela Nilsson Ludvigsson (S), Anders Julin (S), Irene Jonsson (S), Heidi Hankanen Ängberg (C), and Sanna Dabolins (V) propose that the proposal be rejected.

Decision-making process: The chairperson puts the proposals to a vote and determines that the Municipal Board decides to approve Jan-Erik Wallin and others' proposal for a decision.

Voting: A vote is requested.

The following voting outcome is approved: Yes vote for approving Jan-Erik Wallin and others' proposal to refer the proposal to the municipal management office for preparation. No vote for approving Anton César and others' proposal to reject the proposal.

Voting result: With 7 yes votes and 8 no votes, the chairperson determines that the Municipal Board decides, according to Anton César and others' proposal, to reject the proposal.

The voting result is at the end of this section.

Decision: The proposal is rejected.

Municipal Board Minutes

2026-06-15

Extract Confirmation

Note Jan-Erik Wallin (M), Jimmy Lanevik (M), Karin Schagerlind (M), Lady France Mulumba (KD) and Jens Christian Berlin (L) submitted the following written note: It is difficult to understand the objections to this initiative, as the underlying problem described is shared by several parties and has also been raised in previous initiatives concerning the Nolhaga area. There is already significant pressure on the area related to traffic, parking, and accessibility, which is particularly noticeable during major events and through the extensive daily activities involving sports, associations, recreation, and exercise. This initiative does not propose the construction of a parking facility, but rather aims to promptly investigate possible solutions, capacity, costs, and feasibility before any future decisions are made. Therefore, opposing the investigation itself risks appearing as if one is choosing to ignore a problem that is already well-known. We believe that the Nolhaga area needs a long-term, comprehensive approach, but long-term planning should not be used as an excuse to avoid addressing concrete and recurring problems in everyday life. It must be possible to have two perspectives simultaneously: to develop an overall strategy for the entire Nolhaga area, and at the same time, investigate concrete solutions to the accessibility problems that associations, visitors, and businesses already experience. We also believe that it is more responsible to explore the possibility of improving existing paved areas than to risk having to take additional green spaces in the future. For the Moderate Party, the Christian Democrats, and the Liberals, this is about combining long-term vision with action and ensuring that Nolhaga continues to function as an accessible and attractive meeting place for the entire Alingsås.

Anton César (S), Anita Hedén Unosson (S), Peter Norlander (S), Pamela Nilsson Ludvigsson (S), Anders Julin (S) and Irene Jonsson (S) submitted the following note: The Social Democrats agree that the parking situation in the Nolhaga area needs to be addressed and developed as the area grows and is used by more residents, associations, and visitors. However, we voted against the initiative because the municipal board has already made a decision to develop a comprehensive strategy for the Nolhaga area following an initiative from the Social Democrats. This mandate explicitly includes analyzing issues related to traffic, accessibility, safety, parking, natural values, and the area's continued development. According to the municipal administration's assessment, the strategy was to be reported back to the municipal board during the fourth quarter of 2025, but no such report has yet been received. When the municipal board approved the initiative, it also clearly stated that the development of the Nolhaga area should be characterized by a holistic perspective where different interests are weighed before new decisions are made. Against this background, it is remarkable that the municipality is first promoting a new detailed plan for a preschool in the area and is now proposing to investigate the feasibility of a parking garage, without the municipal board having the opportunity to review the comprehensive analysis that has already been commissioned. We believe that this risks leading to continued piecemeal planning, where individual projects and investments are assessed separately, rather than based on a comprehensive assessment of how traffic, parking, associations, sports, recreation, natural values, and future development affect each other. The municipal board should first take a position on the strategy it has itself commissioned before making further decisions that risk affecting the area's long-term development. At the same time, we want to emphasize that we have long been aware of the parking challenges in the area. Even when the new ice rink was inaugurated and...

Municipal Board Minutes

2026-06-15

Confirmation of Extract

The parking situation has become increasingly strained. One of our members proposed improving the gravel area as a temporary parking solution. This proposal was implemented and has become a much-appreciated addition of parking spaces, especially during sporting events, tournaments, and other large events in the area.

For the Social Democrats, it is self-evident that issues related to traffic, accessibility, and parking need to be addressed. However, we believe that such solutions should be implemented within the framework of the overall strategy for the Nolhaga area, which the municipal board has already commissioned and is still waiting to receive.

Decision-Making Documents:

  • § 110 KS - Initiative proposal to the municipal board regarding an investigation into expanded parking capacity at Nolhaga - Jan-Erik Wallin (M) and others.
  • Initiative proposal to the municipal board - Investigation into expanded parking capacity at Nolhaga - Jan-Erik Wallin (M) and others.

Municipal Council Minutes

2026-06-15

Confirmation of Extract

§ 115 2026.265 KS Strategic Directions for Regional Public Transport Description of the Matter The Västra Götaland Region (VGR) has decided to revise the "Vision for Rail 2035" document, including the Västtåg investigation. The new strategic document will serve as a basis for rail investments, provide guidance for regional transport planning, and identify the need for advocacy efforts regarding infrastructure and other measures that promote the development of regional public transport.

Discussions regarding the document will take place in the regional public transport and community planning forums. Municipalities will have the opportunity to submit written comments to the Gothenburg Region (GR).

In addition to the opportunity to submit written comments, GR's infrastructure and public transport network discussed the discussion document at a joint network meeting in May. GR's proposed written comments regarding the strategic directions will be based on discussions within the networks, the dialogue at the regional planning forum (DKS), and any written comments received.

The Municipal Council's working committee was informed about this matter on June 3, 2026, in item § 88.

The Technical Committee addressed this matter on June 15, 2026, in item § 33, and decided to approve the administration's comments on the strategic directions for regional public transport and proposed that the Municipal Council do the same.

Preparation The Urban Planning Department provided the following statement in a letter dated May 29, 2026:

It is positive that VGR is undertaking this revision to create the conditions for long-term planning and to achieve the goals. The department believes that the recommendations for the revision, as outlined in the evaluation of "Vision for Rail 2035," are relevant. The department particularly emphasizes the importance of highlighting the needs of local community planning and ensuring that the various stakeholders understand each other's circumstances. Collaboration is crucial to connect the entire system so that more people can benefit from it.

In connection with upcoming investigations regarding the development of the railway, community planning and operations will be important areas for Alingsås municipality to collaborate on. Further needs that the department identifies are outlined in Appendix 1, "Comments on Strategic Directions for Regional Public Transport."

The Municipal Management Office proposes that the Municipal Council approve the Technical Committee's proposal for comments on the strategic directions for regional public transport.

Proposal for a Decision at the Meeting Heidi Hankanen Ängberg (C) proposes the following addition to Alingsås municipality's comments on the strategic directions for regional public transport:

The following paragraph should be added after the sentence: "The municipality also believes it is important to account for technological developments that may significantly affect the conditions and needs."

Municipal Board Minutes

2026-06-15

Confirmation of Extract

The municipality of Alingsås would like to emphasize that regional public transport planning must also include rural areas. The possibility of living in rural areas depends on the availability of basic public transport with reasonable access to services, education, employment, and regional hubs.

The following three points are added at the end under the heading "Regarding bus services, Alingsås would particularly like to emphasize:":

  • Rural trips must be evaluated based on accessibility, not solely on passenger volume. Public transport in rural areas serves an important function even where the number of passengers is smaller.
  • On-demand transport needs to be developed as part of the public transport system to improve accessibility in areas where regular services cannot be maintained with a high frequency.
  • The municipality sees a need to review the regulations for on-demand transport so that more residents can use the service.

Decision-Making Process The chairperson put the proposal from the technical committee to a vote, and it was rejected. The municipal board approved the proposal from the technical committee.

The chairperson put Heidi Hankanen Ängberg's supplementary proposal to a vote, and it was rejected. The municipal board approved Heidi Hankanen Ängberg's supplementary proposal.

Decision The municipal board adopted the proposal from the technical committee for input to the strategic choices for regional public transport, as detailed in Appendix 1, with the following changes:

The following paragraph is added after the sentence "The municipality also recognizes the importance of accounting for technological developments that may significantly impact conditions and needs."

Alingsås municipality would like to emphasize that regional public transport planning must also include rural areas. The possibility of living in rural areas depends on the availability of basic public transport with reasonable access to services, education, employment, and regional hubs.

The following three points are added at the end under the heading "Regarding bus services, Alingsås would particularly like to emphasize:":

  • Rural trips must be evaluated based on accessibility, not solely on passenger volume. Public transport in rural areas serves an important function even where the number of passengers is smaller.
  • On-demand transport needs to be developed as part of the public transport system to improve accessibility in areas where regular services cannot be maintained with a high frequency.
  • The municipality sees a need to review the regulations for on-demand transport so that more residents can use the service.

To be sent to: The Urban Planning Department

Municipal Board Minutes

2026-06-15

Extract Confirmation

§ 116 2026.021 KS Frameworks for the Municipality of Alingsås 2027-2029 Description of the Issue The governance model for the Municipality of Alingsås was adopted by the Municipal Council on November 6, 2024, § 149. The governance model states that the municipality's framework planning takes place in May, with a decision made by the Municipal Board in June. The framework planning meeting was held on May 13, 2026, and included the Municipal Board's working committee and the group leaders of the parties represented in the Municipal Council.

Preparation The Municipal Administration Office provided the following statement in a letter dated June 2, 2026:

The proposed frameworks suggest that the general increase in municipal funding should be 100 percent of the municipal price index (PKV) in 2027, 100 percent of the PKV in 2028, and 100 percent of the PKV in 2029. Furthermore, full volume compensation is proposed based on the municipality's resource allocation model. The proposed frameworks also include the allocation of general state grants for activity requirements (SN), teacher teaching time (BUN/KUN), ten-year primary school (BUN), and special education (BUN) to the relevant committees. Finally, it is proposed that the Municipal Board receive an additional permanent allocation of 5 million SEK to cover increased local costs related to the new municipal building.

The proposed frameworks are detailed in the attachment to this agenda item.

The budget preparation for the autumn will take place on September 8-9, with the final decision made by the Municipal Council on November 4.

The Municipal Board's working committee addressed this issue on June 3, 2026, § 83, and proposed that the frameworks for 2027-2029 be approved.

Proposed Decision for the Meeting Jan-Erik Wallin (M), Anton César (S), Anita Hedén Unosson (S), Thomas Thim (M), Gunilla Gomér (SD), Sanna Dabolins (V), Heidi Hankanen Ängberg (C), Lady France Mulumba (KD), Jens Christian Berlin (L), and Janine Alm Ericson (MP) attended today's meeting. They proposed the following two additions to the working committee's proposed decision:

  1. The Health and Social Care Committee is proposed to receive 13 million SEK in 2027 to develop the use of welfare technology and strengthen staffing.
  2. The Children and Youth Committee is proposed to receive 12 million SEK in 2027 to prepare for upcoming reforms, adapt premises to future needs, and strengthen efforts to ensure that more students are present in school.

Decision-Making Process The chairperson put the working committee's proposed decision to a vote and determined that the Municipal Board approved the working committee's proposed decision.

15

Municipal Board Minutes

2026-06-15

Extract Confirmation

The chairperson rejected Jan-Erik Wallin and others' supplementary proposal and determined that the municipal board approves Jan-Erik Wallin and others' supplementary proposal.

Decision

The proposed framework for the municipality's committees for 2027-2029 is approved, with the following two additions:

  1. The Health and Care Committee is proposed to receive 13 million SEK in 2027 to develop the use of welfare technology and strengthen staffing.
  2. The Children and Youth Committee is proposed to receive 12 million SEK in 2027 to prepare for upcoming reforms, adapt premises to future needs, and strengthen efforts to increase student attendance in schools.

Distributed to: Municipal Management Office (Finance), all committees

Decision-making documents:

  • §83 KSAU Frameworks 2027-2029 for the Municipality of Alingsås
  • Frameworks 2027-2029 for the Municipality of Alingsås, updated
  • Service Note - Frameworks 2027-2029 for the Municipality of Alingsås

Municipal Board Minutes

2026-06-15

Extract Confirmation

§ 117 2026.269 KS Investment Grant for Renovation - Holmalund City FF Description of the Matter The association Holmalund City FF, registration number 864000-1015 (hereinafter referred to as "the association"), has applied for an investment grant to renovate the locker rooms at Brogårdsvallen, which have been damaged by frost and water. The renovation also includes the fence around the field, the kiosk building, and parts of the seating area.

The past winter has caused significant damage to the facilities at Brogårdsvallen. Cold temperatures, ice formation, and the storm "Dave" have led to extensive moisture and frost damage in the locker rooms, which are currently unusable. The fence around the 11-a-side field, the kiosk building, and parts of the seating area have also been damaged and need to be repaired to ensure the area is safe and functions as intended. A technical assessment from Brixly estimates the cost of demolition and restoration of the locker rooms (to their current condition) at 355,550 SEK excluding VAT, including approximately 50,000 SEK in drying costs. New surface finishes are estimated at 172,000 SEK excluding VAT. For the remaining damage to the fence, roof, kiosk, and seating area, the association estimates that the total costs could amount to approximately 2 million SEK including VAT, but a complete and accurate cost picture can only be determined when the entire damage is exposed and everything has been removed. The association is working to obtain more quotes for the project.

The total budget for the project is 1.8 million SEK. The association is applying for 400,000 SEK from the municipality, with the remaining amount to be financed through grants from SISU and the Sparbanksstiftelsen foundation, as well as the association's own funds.

The association previously applied for and was granted investment grants totaling 5.5 million SEK from the municipal board for a new artificial turf field. Before the formation of Holmalund City FF, Holmalunds IF was granted a number of investment grants from the municipality, including the replacement of an artificial turf field, the replacement of the roof of the clubhouse, and the construction of "Mötesplats Holmalyckan" (a meeting place). In total, Holmalunds IF was granted approximately 2.8 million SEK.

Preparation The municipal administration office provided the following statement in a letter dated May 26, 2026:

The association's application for an investment grant is assessed in accordance with the municipal board's guidelines for investment grants, adopted on June 7, 2023, § 101. The guidelines specify what types of projects an association can receive funding for, what supporting documents must be included in the application, what conditions must or should be met, and what types of associations are not eligible for the grant. Furthermore, the guidelines state that the municipality's investment grants should not exceed half of the total investment cost. In addition, all applications are processed on a rolling basis and decided by the municipal board based on the municipality's total financial resources.

The municipal board is the decision-making body, but has delegated the authority to decide on investment grants with a value of up to a maximum of 100,000 SEK.

The municipal administration office finds that the application amount is less than 50 percent of the total investment, which is in line with the guidelines. The association is awaiting confirmation from other funding sources.

Municipal Board Minutes

2026-06-15

Confirmation of Extract

The existing guidelines do not specify the number or size of investment grants that an individual association can receive. However, it should be noted that the previous investment grant of 5.5 million kronor for a new artificial turf field was something the municipality supported, as it was a joint project aimed at increasing the number of artificial turf fields in the municipality. The application in this case represents 22% of the project's total budget, which the municipality views positively.

The Municipal Management Office therefore believes that the association's application is in line with the guidelines, that the association meets the formal requirements of the guidelines, and that the application should therefore be approved.

The Municipal Board's working committee addressed this matter on June 3, 2026, in item 89.

Decision The association Holmalund City FF (org. no. 864000-1015) is granted an investment grant of up to half of the association's total expenditure for the investment covered by this application, but a maximum of 400,000 kronor, provided that the association, either alone or together with other co-financiers, contributes to the complete funding.

To be sent to: Municipal Management Office (finance), Holmalund City FF

Decision Documentation:

  • §89 KSAU Investment grant for renovation - Holmalund City FF
  • Service memo - Investment grant for renovation - Holmalund City FF
  • Application for investment grant

18

Municipal Board Minutes

2026-06-15

Confirmation of Extract

§ 118 2026.271 KS SAMLA Funding for 2027

Description of the Matter On September 6, 2017, the Municipal Council of Alingsås Municipality, in resolution § 176, approved the interim collaboration in healthcare between Lerum and Alingsås. The "Vårdsamverkan Lerum Alingsås" (SAMLA) collaboration was established in 2017 and is intended for residents who require coordinated and simultaneous healthcare services. It includes services from both the municipality and the region. The services target both the elderly and children and young people, with particular attention given to children and young people, mental health and substance abuse, and elderly individuals requiring end-of-life care. The collaboration within SAMLA includes the region's healthcare services and the Southern Älvsborg Hospital, municipal healthcare, social services, and school health services in the municipalities of Alingsås and Lerum, as well as the Health and Medical Care Board of the West region.

The political consultation group for SAMLA includes representatives from the care and welfare board, the children and youth board, and the social welfare board of Alingsås Municipality.

On June 21, the political consultation group for SAMLA made a preliminary decision to approve the funding for the SAMLA collaboration for 2027 and proposes that the relevant boards and committees confirm the consultation group's decision.

Preparation The Municipal Management Office, in a letter dated May 25, 2026, provided the following statement:

The municipality of Alingsås's share of the funding is allocated from the budget of the care and welfare board, and the cost has been reconciled with the care and welfare administration.

The Municipal Management Office has no further comments.

The Municipal Board's working committee addressed this matter on June 3, 2026, in resolution § 87.

Decision The decision of the SAMLA consultation group to approve the funding for 2027, amounting to 2,038,193 SEK, of which Alingsås Municipality's share is 505,551 SEK, is confirmed.

To be sent to: SAMLA, the care and welfare administration. 19

Municipal Council Minutes

June 15, 2026

Extract Confirmation

Supporting Documents for Decisions:

  • §87: KSAU SAMLA – Budget Allocation for 2027
  • Service Letter – SAMLA Budget Allocation for 2027
  • SAMLA Budget Allocation for the year 2027

20

Municipal Council Minutes

2026-06-15

Confirmation of Extract

§ 119 2026.275 KS Clarification of Parking for Elected Officials Description of the Issue In connection with the move to a new municipal building, the issue of parking for elected officials has become relevant.

Parking at the Kristina 7 block (located on Kungsgatan, adjacent to Väktaregården and Kungsgatan 7-9) and at the new municipal building has been subject to a fee of 5 SEK per hour from 7:00 AM to 6:00 PM since May 1, 2026. It is therefore a public parking area and not a visitor parking area for the municipal properties. Kungsgatan 7-9 will be leased to an external tenant after renovation.

Preparation The Municipal Management Office provided the following statement in a letter dated May 26, 2026:

Parking at the Kristina 7 block has been allocated to the departments with offices in the area since 2011, and the central coordination group adopted parking conditions on January 25, 2011. The following order of priority has been applied for the allocation of parking spaces: company cars, the need for a personal car for work, employees with disabilities, visitors, and other staff. There have been more parking permits than available spaces, and parking spaces have been allocated on a first-come, first-served basis. Elected officials have been able to obtain visitor parking permits to park in the area during meetings and other official duties, subject to availability.

The parking area has been accessible based on the above priorities, with either a permanent or temporary parking permit based on the above priorities. The possibility of obtaining a permanent or temporary parking permit has been discontinued since May 1, 2026, in connection with the move to the new municipal building.

Compensation and allowances for elected officials are regulated by the "Regulations for Compensation to Elected Officials," adopted by the Municipal Council on February 22, 2023, § 3.

Regarding employees, the work is based on a workplace and a work location. Travel and expenses related to travel to and from this location are not reimbursed. For employees traveling on business, the "Guidelines for Business Travel in Alingsås Municipality," adopted by the Municipal Council on February 5, 2024, § 20, apply. Elected officials are encouraged to follow these guidelines where applicable. The right to reimbursement for parking fees for business travel is regulated by the "Mileage Reimbursement Agreement (BIA)" from the Swedish municipalities and regions. According to the BIA, reimbursement for parking fees may be applicable if travel by car is the only option.

For elected officials, the work location is considered to be the location where the meeting is held in the municipality of Alingsås. In the "Regulations for Compensation to Elected Officials" for 2023-2026 and in the draft "Regulations for Compensation to Elected Officials" for 2027-2030, § 9 states that the cost of travel to and from meetings or similar events is covered according to the principles established in the municipal travel agreements for employees (Trakt, BIA, and tax legislation). The Municipal Management Office believes that this means that costs for travel to and from meetings are covered.

Municipal Board Minutes

2026-06-15

Confirmation of Extract

Expenses for attending meetings in Alingsås municipality, such as parking fees, are generally not reimbursed, with the exception of mileage, which is specifically regulated in § 9. Expenses for travel to and from meetings or similar events outside the municipality are reimbursed according to the municipal travel agreements, meaning that mileage and reimbursement for parking fees may be granted.

There are ample parking options within a reasonable distance of the new municipal building for those who do not wish to pay a parking fee.

The Municipal Management Office believes that the "Regulations for Reimbursements to Elected Officials," the "Guidelines for Business Travel in Alingsås Municipality," and the "BIA" (presumably a local document) indicate that no reimbursement is provided for parking fees incurred when traveling to and from meetings within the municipality. The Municipal Management Office proposes that the clarification regarding parking arrangements for elected officials be adopted.

The Municipal Board's working group addressed this matter in item § 90 on June 3, 2026, and proposed that the clarification regarding parking arrangements for elected officials be adopted.

Proposal for a decision at the meeting: Jens Christian Berlin (L) proposes, firstly, rejection, and secondly, an alternative interpretation of the regulations: The "Regulations for Reimbursements" § 9 should be interpreted to mean that if a paid parking space must be used when traveling to and from a meeting or similar event, the parking fee should be reimbursed.

Decision-making process: The chairperson puts the Municipal Board's working group's proposal to a vote against Jens Christian Berlin's proposal for rejection and finds that the Municipal Board approves the Municipal Board's working group's proposal.

The chairperson then puts Jens Christian Berlin's proposal for interpretation to a vote against rejection and finds that the Municipal Board's working group rejects Jens Christian Berlin's proposal for interpretation.

Decision: The clarification regarding parking arrangements for elected officials is adopted. 22

Municipal Board Minutes

2026-06-15

Confirmation of Extract

Dissenting Opinion Jens Christian Berlin (L) submits the following written dissenting opinion: In my opinion, the proposal entitled "Clarification of Parking for Elected Officials" is incorrect. The proposal for a decision should have been rejected, or alternatively, KSAU should have proposed a clearly formulated interpretation of the existing regulations. It is unclear which part of the service document the municipal board is proposed to adopt. Furthermore, the interpretation of the existing regulations is not correct.

Provisions regarding travel allowances are found in the regulations for allowances to elected officials. According to § 9, paragraph 1 of the regulations, costs for travel to and from meetings or similar events shall be reimbursed according to the principles established in the municipal travel agreements for employees, including the BIA (Car Allowance Agreement). The second paragraph states that a prerequisite for reimbursement is that the travel distance from the elected official's residence exceeds five kilometers. According to § 3, appendix 1 of the BIA, if a paid parking space must be used, the employee is reimbursed for the amount paid.

The municipal management office's opinion that the BIA should not apply to travel from the elected official's home to meetings in Alingsås municipality contradicts the direct wording in the regulations. It is "costs for travel to and from meetings or similar events" that should be reimbursed according to, among other things, the BIA. It is clear from the regulations that travel from the home is what is meant. A contrary interpretation would mean that travel from the home to meetings should be reimbursed according to rules that exclude reimbursement from the home itself. In such a case, mileage reimbursement cannot be granted based on the BIA. In my opinion, it is clear that the regulations govern which trips are eligible for reimbursement, while the actual reimbursement is governed by, among other things, the BIA.

The fact that the municipal management office in the service document mixes the interpretation of the existing regulations with the statement that there are plenty of parking options for those who do not want to pay a parking fee, is a sign that they have not succeeded in separating interpretation from application.

Decision-Making Documents • §90 KSAU Clarification of Parking for Elected Officials • Service Document - Clarification regarding parking for elected officials

23

Minutes Extract Confirmation

§ 120 2026.218 KS Alingsås Municipality's Guidelines OPF-KR 26, Regulations Regarding Transition Support, Pension, and Family Protection for Elected Officials Case Description Before each term, a review of compensation for elected officials is conducted. At the beginning of 2026, the group leaders of the parties in the municipal council have been in dialogue about compensation levels and transition support for the term 2027-2030.

On May 11, 2026, the Municipal Board addressed the matter in item § 80 and decided to refer the matter back for further preparation, so that the municipal administration office should provide documentation for a retroactive allocation of pension contributions, given the long processing time during which two years of increased pension contributions have been lost.

Preparation In a letter dated May 27, 2026, the municipal administration office provided the following statement:

The municipal administration office has assisted the group leaders with an analysis of transition support for elected officials in other municipalities.

The regulations for compensation for elected officials for the years 2027-2030 will be divided into two separate governing documents for the upcoming term. Issues related to pension, transition support, and family protection are often more extensive and are partly governed by recommendations from the Swedish Association of Local Authorities and Regions (SKR). A separate document makes it easier to revise and update these issues as needed.

The board of SKR adopted a proposal for new regulations regarding transition support, pension, and family protection for elected officials (OPF-KR 26) on December 19, 2025. These regulations apply to assignments that begin after the election or later and are divided into four chapters: introductory provisions, transition support, pension regulations, and family protection. A number of changes are being introduced. A new rule excludes transition support in cases of a prison sentence of two years or more. Time spent with other employers can be included in the qualifying period, with coordination of all income from the first year, with a deductible amount corresponding to one-twelfth of the base amount. Regarding pensions, the concept of cohabitation is clarified, among other things.

The administration has prepared a proposal that is largely based on SKR's proposal (OPF-KR 26). In addition, based on the dialogue with the group leaders, a regulation is introduced stating that an elected official should not be granted transition support if the elected official has acted in a way that seriously affects public trust and damages the municipality's reputation. In such cases, decisions require a qualified majority in the municipal council.

A position as an elected official is an important part of the democratic structure and requires adapted conditions. According to the Municipal Act (2017:725), Chapter 4, Section 1, an elected official is a person who has been elected to a position by the municipal council, board, or other municipal authority.

A position as an elected official is not an employment and the elected official therefore does not have an employment relationship with the municipality. Labor laws that regulate, for example, working hours, vacation, and employment protection are therefore not applicable.

Municipal Board Minutes

2026-06-15

Extract Confirmation

An elected official who holds a full-time political office is, in certain respects, treated as an employee, for example, regarding occupational health services.

Since there is no employment relationship, the elected official cannot be covered by regulations applicable to civil servants. Pension benefits, according to the Income Tax Act (1999:1299), can only be provided within the framework of an employment relationship.

The political office also involves operating in an environment where various societal interests need to be balanced. This can, in some cases, create a certain distance from the local job market. Against this background, it is reasonable to offer support to those leaving a political office to help them find their way back into working life. Given that elected officials have different backgrounds and varying circumstances, it is important to ensure that adequate support and re-entry opportunities are available.

The transition regulations aim for an active transition through targeted measures and time-limited financial support, with the goal of increasing the individual's opportunities to develop and advance in their career after the term of office.

The Municipal Board decided to refer the matter back to the Municipal Management Office, which should provide documentation for a retroactive allocation of pension contributions. The administration has investigated the possibility of making the allocation retroactively and has found that this is possible. The costs associated with this are detailed in the financial assessment.

The Municipal Management Office proposes that the Municipal Council adopt the draft application guidelines for Alingsås Municipality, OPF-KR 26 - Regulations on transition support, pension, and family protection for elected officials, with a retroactive pension allocation from January 1, 2025, until the new regulations come into effect on January 1, 2027.

The Municipal Board's Executive Committee discussed the matter on June 3, 2026, in item 85.

Decision Proposed decision for the Municipal Council The application guidelines for Alingsås Municipality, OPF-KR 26 - Regulations on transition support, pension, and family protection for elected officials, are adopted, with a retroactive pension allocation from January 1, 2025, until the new regulations come into effect on January 1, 2027. 25

Municipal Council Minutes

2026-06-15

Confirmation of Extract

Extract Confirmation

§ 122 Submitted Proposals Description of the matter According to the Municipal Act, Chapter 4, Section 20, each member of the board has the right to submit a proposal.

On June 15, 2026, Anton César (S), Anita Hedén Unosson (S), Peter Norlander (S), Pamela Nilsson Ludvigsson (S), Anders Julin (S), and Irene Jonsson (S) submitted a proposal to the Municipal Board regarding strengthening the employee perspective in political decision-making documents.

The proposal suggests that the Municipal Board decide:

  1. To assign the municipal administration office the task of developing a common municipal procedure for how the employee perspective should be presented in decision-making documents prior to political decisions.
  2. That the procedure should be applied in all committees and companies to the extent that decisions may affect the work environment, organization, or employment conditions.

Decision The proposal is received and will be included on the agenda for the next meeting of the Municipal Board.

28
tldr: The municipality of Alingsås has approved the budget for the fire and rescue services in Alingsås and Vårgårda for the years 2027-2029. The funds will be used, among other things, for new fire trucks and new radio systems.
lattlast:
The municipal council has made a decision regarding the finances of the Alingsås and Vårgårda Fire and Rescue Service Association. This means that the municipality of Alingsås will pay a certain amount each year to cover the costs of the fire department and rescue services.

In the year 2027, the municipality will pay almost 70 million Swedish krona to the association. This amount will increase slightly each year until the ninth year. These funds are intended to cover the regular operations and the increased costs associated with new technical communication systems.

There is also a plan for major equipment purchases up to the year 2031. Among the most important items to be purchased are a new aerial ladder truck in 2027 and a new fire engine in 2028. New vehicles, boats, and water pumps will also be acquired.

This decision ensures that the fire and rescue service has a plan for how to use its funds to continue operating safely. It is about ensuring that they have the right vehicles and equipment to assist citizens in emergency situations.
